overview: The Marathon County Board held an adjourned organizational meeting where committee appointments were approved, two at-large members were elected to the Executive Committee, and a resolution was unanimously passed to reduce landfill tipping fees for debris from the recent severe weather disaster affecting Ringle and other communities. The fee reduction from $65 to approximately $36.94 per ton will remain in effect through June 17th, with the state and town of Ringle waiving their portions of the fee.

agenda.item: Pledge of Allegiance and moment of reflection
Roll call (technical difficulties with meeting manager system)
Committee appointments - Chair remarks and goals
Election of at-large Executive Committee members
Resolution 24-26: Out of home placement reserve increase
Resolution 26-26: Landfill tipping fee modification for disaster debris

discussions.body: Chair Gibbs outlined goals for committee appointments developed with Vice Chair Boots, including increasing diversity of perspectives, ensuring leadership development for newer supervisors (34% are in first or second term), and honoring supervisor preferences. The motion to approve both county board chair and administrator appointments was made by Supervisor Cavelli, seconded by Supervisor Crims. The motion carried but was not unanimous, with Supervisor Mask unable to vote due to technical issues.

discussions.item: Election of At-Large Executive Committee Members
discussions.body: Three supervisors were nominated: Supervisor Hapa (nominated by Supervisor Mask), Supervisor Leur (nominated by Supervisor Rosenberg), and Supervisor Arstead (nominated by Vice Chair Boots). Supervisor Rosenberg explained her nomination of Supervisor Leur, citing her advocacy for the county's youngest and oldest residents. Paper ballots were distributed and supervisors selected two of the three candidates. Supervisors Hapa and Arstead were elected to the Executive Committee.

Administrator Leonard and Solid Waste Director Hagen Busher presented a proposal from the Solid Waste Management Board to reduce tipping fees for disaster debris from $65 to approximately $36.94 per ton. The state is waiving $13.10 and the town of Ringle is waiving $2.33. Public comment was allowed by special dispensation for Jessica Toasty and Matt Haybeck, both requesting Marathon County wave its fees entirely. Toasty noted 44 homes affected and requested a six-month waiver. Multiple supervisors asked clarifying questions about fee structures, timelines, and future policy. The board first voted unanimously to suspend rules to consider the resolution, then passed Resolution 26-26 unanimously.

publicComment: Two residents were granted special dispensation to speak on the landfill tipping fee resolution. Jessica Toasty argued that Marathon County should waive fees entirely as the state, DNR, and Ringle have done, noting that private businesses have donated up to $40,000 in cleanup efforts and residents are facing significant out-of-pocket expenses. She requested a six-month fee waiver. Matt Haybeck spoke on behalf of affected homeowners, noting some of the approximately 44 damaged homes are still awaiting insurance adjusters and requested more time for the fee waiver.
actionItems: Resolution 26-26 approved: Landfill tipping fees reduced to approximately $36.94 per ton for certified disaster debris through June 17, 2025
Supervisors Hapa and Arstead elected to Executive Committee at-large positions
Administrator Leonard to explore partnership with Community Foundation for reimbursing uninsured disposal costs
Solid Waste Management Board to develop formal policy on disaster-related fee reductions within six months
Resolution 24-26 approved: Out of home placement reserve increase
Resolution 25-26 approved: Social services budget amendment
title: Marathon County Regular Meeting
